name: Frontend redesign dispatch clarity
description: When dispatching a lead for frontend redesign work, ensure the objective clearly distinguishes between core layout changes (masonry) and secondary improvements (search bar styling, infinite scroll). Leads may prioritize different aspects based on their reading of the spec. Consider breaking large redesigns into multiple work streams if they have independent components.

name: Premature lead closure recovery
description: When a lead closes its main issue prematurely while spawned builders are still working, create a new parent issue and spawn a new lead to coordinate completion. This maintains proper tracking and ensures the work stream has clear ownership.

content: Masonry layout with CSS columns works best with variable-height cards using width:100%, height:auto, and min-height on cards to ensure reasonable sizing. Object-fit:cover should be avoided to preserve full image aspect ratios.

name: Resuming paused work with new lead
description: When resuming paused work where the original lead has completed, spawn a new lead with a different name (e.g., lead-v3-integration instead of lead-v3-implementation) to avoid confusion. Provide clear context about previous progress and the need to resume from saved state. Check builder branches for completed work that needs integration.
